mxCell.value: Candidate added to one or multiple job openings

Identity and account access

  • register/login
  • account creation
  • account region selection
  • invitations
  • roles and permissions
  • switch active account

ATS core

  • create/edit job opening
  • create/edit pipeline
  • ordered statuses per pipeline
  • create/import candidate
  • assign candidate skills from predefined catalog
  • create application
  • move candidate through statuses
  • track status history

UX and collaboration

  • pipeline board
  • drag-and-drop
  • realtime updates (kanban view --> websockets)
  • search/filter
  • candidate detail panels
Sourcing
  • search external providers
  • normalize provider results
  • import sourced candidates

Supporting capabilities

  • CV upload
  • CV parsing
  • notifications
  • integrations
  • audit
  • analytics
  • compliance actions

Service ownership rule

Each service owns its own data.
No other service reads that service’s DB directly.
Cross-service access happens through:

  • service APIs
  • Async communication is done events base with messaging systems like Kafka

Service regional rule

For tenant isolation and regional compliance, account related services and data are kept regional

Auth Service
(login, pwd check, token issuance, session logic, MFA)

User Service
(user profile, name, display name, email)

Account & Membership
(account creation, region & accout mapping, invitations, membership mgmt --> users & accout mapping)

RBAC service
(user roles and permissions, route authorization checks)

Skills service
(skills catalog, crud skills)

Pipeline Service
crud pipelines, ordered status per pipeline

Candidate Service
create/import candidate, assign skills, candidate detail panels

Web BFF
(SSR page shell for web, session handling, UI aggregation, shaping response for web screens check, token issuance, session logic, MFA)

Mobile BFF
(shaping response for mobile)

Realtime Gateway Service
(Kanban live updates: websockets connections, channel subscriptions, push live updates)
is_system (predefined roles -> admin, recruiter, hiring_manager, viewer)
We would seed default pipeline statuses when a new pipeline is created
Skills service
Set secure HTTP-only cookie + SSR Ready session

DatabasesRules

  • Where read access is high we include a caching layer. E.g.: Jobs
  • We avoid single point of failure by having master and read replicas (read replicas can also be used to split the read load)

POST /v1/accounts Authorization: Bearer <token> Content-Type: application/json
{ "name": "ZenZenCorp Singapore", "home_region": "ap-southeast-1" }

POST /v1/accounts Authorization: Bearer <token> Content-Type: application/json
{ "name": "ZenZenCorp Singapore", "home_region": "ap-southeast-1" }

POST /v1/applications Authorization: Bearer <scoped-token> Idempotency-Key: 0dc6f73e-bd8d-46ee-b5f8-7c06d7eecdf8 Content-Type: application/json
{ "candidate_id": "me_123", "job_id": "Director of Platform Engineering" }

POST /v1/jobs Authorization: Bearer <scoped-token> Content-Type: application/json
{ "title": "Senior Backend Engineer", "department": "Engineering", "location": "Singapore", "pipeline_id": "pipe_eng_default" }

POST /v1/applications/{application_id}/move-status Authorization: Bearer <scoped-token> Idempotency-Key: 52f9f6e7-2bba-4253-96a9-28b0bc6f9c1b Content-Type: application/json
{ "to_status_id": "home_assignment", "expected_version": 7 }

Notes
  • Idempotency-Key: protects against duplicate action with user retries
  • expected_version: protects against concurrent silent overwrites

4.1 Performance and fast rendering
  • CDN for images and static content --> multi-region caching --> very fast rendering
  • Async communications: backend operations do not block the user interactions with the UI
  • SSR (server side rendering) for speed and security & improve SEO
    • public career pages
    • public job detail pages
    • secure authenticated shell/bootstrap where useful
  • CSR for pages with dynamic content & low SEO value
    • pipeline board (very dynamic)
    • candidate detail side panels
    • drag n drop interactions
    • search & filtering
  • Candidate and job caching for speed (candidate data and job data are expected to have low write but heavy reads)
4.2 User Experience 
  • optimistic drag n drop: visual update position before the server side process the change
  • virtualized long columns: render only the subset of cards currently visible within the viewport when number of card in a column is too big (30+) --> includes dynamic loading as the user scroll. 
     It will be useful when many candidate applying to one job.
  • Use side panels to show candidate detail or job details when clicking on a user card or the job for which the pipeline is for
  • Easy button to call for action such as "notify hiring manager" when a reminder is needed.  
  • Live updates for other users: Use of websockets for realtime changes and collaboration
  • Query invalidation / background refresh: Even with websockets, the client should be able to refresh a stale view for recovery
  • Reconnect and fallback: If websockets disconnects, reconnection should happen automatically and refetch the kanban board. If it keeps failing due to unstable network it could fallback to polling.
4.3 Pipeline board interaction
Drag candidate card to new status
4.4 Websockets: where, why and how

Where

  • Internal authenticated pipeline board (Kanban)
  • Future collaboration authenticated screens
What they enable
  • near real-time board updates
  • live collaboration
  • avoid frequent polling
WebSocket session/subcription model
0. Extra - Scale estimate for performances and provisioning

5.1 Assumptions

Let's make the following assumption for a design realistic but still scalable

  • 10,000 Accounts
  • ~50 internal users per account
  • ~20,000 candidates per account
  • 500 open + historical job opening per account
  • 40 application per job opening
  • CV size: 500 KB
  • active regions: 5
  • read:write ratio for the UI: ~80:20
  • pipeline board used collaboratively by a smaller subset of users
5.2 Total Scale
  • users: 10,000 accounts x 50 users = 500,000 users
  • candidates: 10,000 accounts x 20,000 candidates = 200m candidates
    • significant size: justifying regional partitioning by itself for faster DB querying
  • job openings: 10,000 accounts x 500 jobs = 5m jobs
  • job applications: 5m jobs x 40 applications/job = 200m applications
    • indexation: 
      • application_id
      • New index: idx_applications_account_job_status
        • fetches all applications for one account + one job
5.3 Storage estimate
  • Candidate Row assuming
    • row + indexes + metadata ~= 2KB
    • skill mapping ~10 skills / candidate + index overhead ~= 1KB
    • 200m candidates x 3KB = ~600GB
    • Assuming 20% is considered active for caching: 120GB memory for caching
  • Application row
    • row + indexes ~= 1KB
    • status history: ~200B + ~8 transition / application --> 1.6KB
    • total: ~2.5KB
    • 200m applicaiton x 2.5KB ~= 500GB
  • Other DBs for Jobs, pipelines, memberships, roles, etc. have low number of records relatively
  • TOTAL: ~1 to 2 TB across all regions
5.4 File Storage estimate
  • 200m candidate x 1 resume per candidate x 500KB (average resume size)
  • => 100 TB
  • If some candidates have multiple attachments it can grow quickly
  • Conclusion: Object storage is a must
5.5 Request throughput estimate
  • Activity assumption
    • 10% of 500k users are active daily = 50k daily active users
    • peak concurrent internal recruiters, let's say 5,000 to 10,000
    • not all use Kanban pipeline board at once
  • ATS API traffic
    • Assuming each active recruiter generates
      • a few searches / minute
      • periodic page loads
      • occasional pipeline moves
  • Search Traffic
    • Suppose 10k active users at peak and each does 1 search every 20s
    • 10,000 / 20 = 500 searches / sec at peak
    • might be worth considering a search service with OpenSearch for fast querying
  • Pipeline moves
    • Suppose 10k active users
    • only 5% are moving candidates actively
    • each active movers does 1 move every 30s
    • 10,000 * 5% / 30 = ~17 writes / second
    • Considering burst potentials we could imagine 50~100 moves/sec at peak

Pipeline Service
crud pipelines, ordered status per pipeline

Candidate Service
create/import candidate, assign skills, candidate detail panels
